title: runlabel: run any command
body:
As discussed [1], the runlabel command should execute any command
specified in a label.  The reasoning behind is that we cannot restrict
which options are passed to Podman which thereby has full access to the
host (runlabels must be used with care).

With the updated semantics, runlabel will substitute the commands with a
basepath equal to "docker" or "podman" with "/proc/self/exe", and
otherwise leave the command unchanged to execute any other command on
the host.

[1] https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1607#issuecomment-428321382

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

Makes sense, thanks for the explanation. I will update the PR.

On Fri 12. Oct 2018 at 15:02, Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> *@rhatdan* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In cmd/podman/shared/funcs.go
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1637#discussion_r224775680>:
>
> > @@ -15,8 +16,20 @@ func GenerateCommand(command, imageName, name string) []string {
>  		name = imageName
>  	}
>  	cmd := strings.Split(command, " ")
> -	// Replace the first element of cmd with "/proc/self/exe"
> -	newCommand = append(newCommand, "/proc/self/exe")
> +
> +	// Replace the first element of cmd with "/proc/self/exe" if "podman"
>
> If I setup a container to run on special/script/podman or
> special/script/docker which has enhanced commands, then I would find it
> aggravating that there would be no way to run the script, other then to
> rename it to a non special name.

@vrothberg would you be willing to also add a runlabel API endpoint for varlink? I can help you get started/learn about varlink if you like ...

> @vrothberg would you be willing to also add a runlabel API endpoint for varlink? I can help you get started/learn about varlink if you like ...

Sure, I can do that and thanks for the offer, @baude. I'll ping you in case I get stuck :+1:

> If we are going to allow commands, then I think an integration test or two would be prudent. Else LGTM

Tests are now there, PTAL @baude. Something we might need to check a bit is the command generation and how to treat strings and escaped sequences. Currently, we split at whitespaces but that could lead to issues when an argument is an entire string, e.g., `foobar="a very long string"`.

> Tests are now there, PTAL @baude. Something we might need to check a bit is the command generation and how to treat strings and escaped sequences. Currently, we split at whitespaces but that could lead to issues when an argument is an entire string, e.g., foobar="a very long string".

I'd like to address this in another PR using https://godoc.org/github.com/google/shlex.
The tests need to go into the e2e tests; I will wrap that up tomorrow.
